r: g̠ain, g̠ain-ě-moʻjama, g̠ain-ě-manqūt̤a, g, r, grasséyé, gh, g̠, abjad
def: (called g̠ain-ě-moʻjama, or g̠ain-ě-manqūt̤a), the twenty-fifth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the twenty-second of the Persian, and the nineteenth of the Arabic). It is a guttural g accompanied by a grating sound as in gargling, of which there is no example in English; — the Northumbrian r, and the French r in grasséyé are approximations to it. It occurs chiefly in Persian and Arabic words; but it is also found in some Hindūstānī words, in which it takes the place of an original ग g, or घ gh. It has no corresponding character in the Devanāgarī alphabet; and hence, when it occurs in words borrowed from the Persian or Arabic, it is usually represented by ग़ g̠. Its numerical value (according to abjad) is 1000.

s.m. = S گميتا गम्यता gamyatā, s.f. Accessibleness; traversableness; attainableness; perceptibility.

ety: for A. غمزة g̠amzat, n. of un. fr. g̠amz, inf. n. of غمز 'to make a sign,' &c.
def: s.m. A sign with the eye, a wink; an amorous glance, ogling; coquetry, affectation
s.m. Singing; a song
subs: s.f. The science of vocal music.

def: s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.

ety: inf. n. of غنی 'to be or become free from want,' &c.
def: s.f. Freedom from need or want; competence, sufficiency; richness, wealthiness; riches, wealth; — content.

ety: gā˚ = Prk. गा(इ) or गाअ(इ) = S. गाय(ति), rt. गै
def: s.m. Singing, &c.
subs: (-ko), To have acquired the faculty of singing, to be able (or to know how) to sing
s.m. Singing and playing; song and music.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गान (gāna).

ety: prob. fr. Zend gaona = S. गुण
def: adj. affix, Fold (it is affixed to numerals, and is often redundant, e.g. panj-gāna, 'five-fold'; 'five').

s.m. Flock, troop, company, host, multitude, number, series; genus; group, class, category; tribe, sect, party; — troops or classes of inferior deities (esp. certain troops of demigods considered as Śiva's attendants and under the special superintendence of the god Gaṇeśa); — a small body of troops, consisting of three gulmas (i.e. twenty-seven chariots, twenty-seven elephants, eighty-one horse, and one hundred and thirty-five foot); — a series or group of asterisms or lunar mansions classed under three heads (that of the gods, that of men, and that of the Rākshasas or demons); — (in Arith.) a number; — (in Prosody) a division of time ( = 30 kasṭhas or about eight seconds); a measure of four instants or short syllables; — (in Gram.) a series of roots or words belonging to the same rule and called after the first word of the series (e.g. ad-ādi, the Gaṇa ad, &c., or the whole series of roots of the second class); — (gaṇ is not unfrequently used as an affix to form plurals, e.g. tārā-gaṇ, The starry host, the stars)
subs: vulg. gaṇ-pat, s.m. 'Lord of the inferior deities,' an epithet of Gaṇeś; — a proper name
s.m. Public property; common stock; — a variety of articles
s.m. pl. The classes of demi-gods or inferior deities who are the attendants of Śiva (see gaṇ)
s.m. The leader or chief of the attendants of any god; the chief of Śiva's attendants, the god Gaṇeśa

vulg. gaṇes, gaṇes (gaṇa + īśa), s.m. lit. 'Lord or chief of the troops' (of inferior deities; see gaṇ); name of the son of Śiva and Pārvatī (he is the god of wisdom, and the remover of obstacles; hence he is invoked at the commencement of all undertakings, literary composition, &c. Most Hindū literary compositions begin with the phrase śrī-gaṇeśāya-namah, 'reverence to the divine Gaṅeś); — a name of the god Śiva; — a proper name (of men)
s.f. A Hindū festival observed in honour of Ganeś on the fourth of Aghan
s.m. 'A cow-dung image of Ganeś'; a fat dullard; — a lubberly fellow; an arrant fool.
ex: اُس کے کوئی اَچّھے گُن ہَیں ہی نَہِیں۔
us ke koī acche gun ha͠i hī nahī̃.
He has no good qualities at all.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit गुण (guṇá).

def: s.m. Plunderers; enemies.
s.m. (f. -ī), One who reckons, a calculator; an arithmetician; — an astrologer; one who calculates (or casts) nativities, &c.
s.f. The wife of an astrologer (see gaṇak).
s.m. Offensive breath; bad smell from the mouth.
(inf. n. of غنم 'to obtain, get, or take spoil'), s.m.(?) Plundering, carrying off; plunder, spoil, booty (syn. g̠anīmat).
s.m. (f. -ā), Reckoning, counting; enumeration; calculation; adding, addition
part. adj. To be counted or reckoned, calculable; requiring counting; to be classed.
s.f. Arithmetic (see also gaṇatva).
s.m. (f. gaṇatā), s.m. The forming a class, or multitude, or assemblage; the belonging to a party or faction; the condition (or the office) of an attendant of Śiva (see gaṇ); — arithmetic.

ety: gannā, q.v.+orī = Prk. उल्लिआ = S. उल+इका
def: s.f. A bulrush; reed-grass.
s.f. = گانڙ gāṅṛ, q.v. s.f. The anus; the privities
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गण्ड (gaṇḍá). Cognate with Assamese গাঁৰ (gãr).

ety: Pehl. gand; Zend gaiñti; S. गन्ध
def: s.f. Smell, bad smell; stink, stench; filth, ordure.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गन्ध (gandha, “smell, stink”). Cognate with Persian گنده (gande, “stenchy”).

def: s.m. (Dakh.) The upper and forepart of the neck (under the jaw).
s.f. contrac. of gāṅḍ or gāṅṛ (used chiefly in comp.).
adj. = گنده ganda, q.v. s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
s.m. (dialec. or rustic) Land adjacent (or lying nearest) to a village (gāʼoṅ; S. grāma).

s.m. An aggregate of four 'cowries,' or four pice (paisā); the number four, counting by fours; — disjunction, separation; a joint; a reed; sugar-cane (syn. gannā); — a ring, circle (esp. a magic circle, or one which is supposed to act as a charm or amulet, i.e. as an 'obstacle or impediment' to the influence of evil spirits, &c.); — a charmed cord (generally of three coloured threads knotted together) bound round the neck (or wrist, or ankle) to avert or remove demoniac influence, or the fascination of an evil eye; — the string which teachers of particular arts or crafts (as singers, dancers, conjurers, &c.) bind round a finger or the wrist of the right arm of their pupils; — a cord of black thread (or large conch shells, or plates of gold and silver of an acuminate heart shape, strung together and) fastened round the neck of a horse as an ornament or an amulet; — the ring or collar round the neck of a turtle-dove or ring-dove; — (local) dependence (e.g. unke āʼe-par kyā gaṇḍā hai); — a mark, spot, stripe
subs: v.t. To charm, exorcise (by means of amulets); to bewitch

def: s.m. = گنڐا gaṇḍā, q.v. s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
adj. (f. -ī), Stinking, fetid; rotten, addled (as an egg), putrid; rancid; — filthy, dirty; — foul, obscene, indecent (speech); — harsh, sour, vile (temper)
subs: adj. & s.m. Having stinking armpits; — one whose armpits emit a stench
s.f. Rain which falls in the cold weather
s.m. Stinking or dirty water; — semen; — urine; — wine
s.f. The having stinking breath; stinking breath
or ganda-biroza, s.m. Olibanum, male frankincense, the resin of the Boswellia thurifera; the crude turpentine or resin of the cīr or pine, P. longifolia
v.t. To make foul or dirty, to foul, to soil; to defile, pollute; to stain, sully, disgrace.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَنْدَه (ganda). First attested in c. 1679 as Middle Hindi گندہ (gndh /⁠ganda⁠/).

def: s.m. The cheek; side of the face; — a boil, a pimple; a goitre, or other excrescence of the neck; — a mark, spot; — a rhinoceros
subs: s.m. The cheek; the temple of an elephant
s.f. & m. Inflammation of the glands of the neck; goitre, bronchocele; — scrofula.

def: s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)

ety: ganda, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Stink, stench; rottenness, putrefaction; — filth; ordure
subs: To strew filth about; to make a place filthy, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گندگی (gandagī).

def: s.m. = gindar, q.v. s.m. A kind of insect which is very destructive to growing 'gram' and arhar.

ety: S. गन्धकः, by change of gender; P. also gandak
def: s.f. Sulphur, brimstone; gunpowder
subs: or gandak-kā tez-āb, Oil of vitriol; vitriolic acid; sulphuric acid

def: s.m. = gaṇḍakī, q.v. s.f. Name of a river in the northern part of India.
s.m. A rhinoceros; — an epithet of the people of Videha, living on the river Gaṇḍakī.
s.m. corr. of gaṇḍak, q.v. s.f. Sulphur, brimstone; gunpowder
subs: or gandak-kā tez-āb, Oil of vitriol; vitriolic acid; sulphuric acid

def: s.f. Name of a river in the northern part of India.
s.f. A kind of grass, Andropogon muricatum (used in thatching; and the root of which, known as ḵẖas-ḵẖas, is used for ṭat̤ṭīs).

ety: old P. gandanāk; perhaps ganda+nāk, qq.v.
def: s.m. A leek, Allium porrum; — barley inserted in onions and sown
s.f. A kind of grass, Andropogon muricatum (used in thatching; and the root of which, known as ḵẖas-ḵẖas, is used for ṭat̤ṭīs).

ety: gaṅḍar = gaṅḍarī = gaṅḍerī, q.v. + ālā = S. आलयः
def: s.m. The receptacle for the sugar-cane at the mill before it is cut up.

ety: gaṅḍar = gaṅḍarī = gaṅḍerī, q.v. + ālā = S. आलयः
def: s.m. The receptacle for the sugar-cane at the mill before it is cut up.
s.f. A Hindū dancing girl.
s.m. = gandaṛwālā, q.v.
s.m. = gaṅḍarālā q.v. s.m. The receptacle for the sugar-cane at the mill before it is cut up.
s.m. A pole-axe; battle-axe; — an instrument (a kind of chopper) for cutting sugar-cane, jawāṛ-stalks, &c. for fodder.
s.m. A rustic form of gaṅḍāsā, q.v.

ety: the preceding, with S. इका in place of कः
def: s.f. A small chopper for cutting sugar-cane, &c. (see ganḍāsā).
s.f. = ganḍerī, gaṛerī, qq.v.
s.f. A joint, or piece, or segment of sugar-cane; — (dialec.) a knot; joint.

ety: S. गन्धं, or गन्धः by change of gender
def: s.f. Smell, odour, scent, perfume, fragrance; bad smell, stink, stench
subs: s.m. The Gardenia florida (ʻindigenous in China, and cultivated throughout India on account of its large white fragrant flowers'); — sandal-wood; — any sweet-smelling flower
s.m. Sandal-wood; — a species of jasmine (cf. gandh-rāj)
s.m. A fragrant wood (as sandal, aloe-wood, &c.)
s.m. Sulphur; — name of a mountain (prob. as being the source of sulphur); — a name of Rāvaṇ; — name of a certain monkey
s.m. 'The wafter of fragrance,' the wind; the zephyr; — adj. Fragrant.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = ganda, q.v.
subs: s.m. corr. of ganda-biroza or fīroza, q.v.s.v. ganda.

def: s.f. (in H. but m. in S.) Sulphur ( = gandak).
wety: From Sanskrit गन्धक (gandhaka, “sulfur”).

def: s.m. The flying (or green) bug (prob. any insect that has an offensive smell).

s.m. The third of the seven primary notes of music; — name of a people and their country, commonly called Kandahār, and lying between the north of India and Persia; — name of one of the princes of that country.
s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode.

s.m. (f. -ī), An aërial minstrel, a kind of celestial musician; a class or (one of a class) of demi-gods who inhabit the heaven of Indra and constitute the orchestra at all the banquets of the principal deities; — the soul after death and previous to its being born again (according to Hindū notions); — a horse; — a kind of deer
subs: s.m. or gandharb-gīt, s.m. The minstrelsy of the celestial choir; — a Gandharb's song
or gandharb-biwāh, or gandharb-byāh, s.m. Marriage from mutual agreement (before witnesses or in writing, and without the usual ceremonies).

def: s.f. = gandharvī.
s.f. A princess of the Gāndhāris (esp. the wife of Dhr̤ita-rāshṭra); — one of the tutelary female deities of the Jains.

s.m. = gandharb, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), An aërial minstrel, a kind of celestial musician; a class or (one of a class) of demi-gods who inhabit the heaven of Indra and constitute the orchestra at all the banquets of the principal deities; — the soul after death and previous to its being born again (according to Hindū notions); — a horse; — a kind of deer
subs: s.m. or gandharb-gīt, s.m. The minstrelsy of the celestial choir; — a Gandharb's song
or gandharb-biwāh, or gandharb-byāh, s.m. Marriage from mutual agreement (before witnesses or in writing, and without the usual ceremonies).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to the Gandharvas; — musical; — a form of marriage which requires only mutual agreement; — the art of the Gandharvas, song, music, dance.

s.m. (f. -ī), An aërial minstrel, a kind of celestial musician; a class or (one of a class) of demi-gods who inhabit the heaven of Indra and constitute the orchestra at all the banquets of the principal deities; — the soul after death and previous to its being born again (according to Hindū notions); — a horse; — a kind of deer
subs: s.m. or gandharb-gīt, s.m. The minstrelsy of the celestial choir; — a Gandharb's song
or gandharb-biwāh, or gandharb-byāh, s.m. Marriage from mutual agreement (before witnesses or in writing, and without the usual ceremonies).

def: fem. of gandharv, q.v.
s.f. The plant 'Lemon grass,' Andropogon schœnanthus (also written gandh-bel, q.v.).
s.m. (f. -inī), A seller of perfumes (as rose-water, ʻat̤r, &c.); a perfumer; — a bug; — the green flying-bug.
s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), A vendor of perfumes, a perfumer; — a kind of worm having a strong fœtid smell, a tree-bug.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having a smell (whether good or bad); scented, odorous, fragrant; stinking, fœtid; — a scented person, &c.
s.m. Name of a vagrant tribe (prob. the same as gaṅḍhū).
s.f. An epithet of the Ganges or of the goddess Gangā ( = gāndinī).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jāṭs.
s.f. Name of a princess of Kāśi (who was the wife of Śvaphalka and mother of Akrūra; — see gāndinī).
s.m. = gandhī, q.v. s.m. (f. -inī), A seller of perfumes (as rose-water, ʻat̤r, &c.); a perfumer; — a bug; — the green flying-bug.
s.m. = gaṇḍ-mālā, q.v.s.v. gaṇḍ. s.f. = گانڙ gāṅṛ, q.v. s.f. The anus; the privities
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright

def: s.f. A circle (esp. that which Rāma drew round Sītā to protect her from Rāvaṇa; — (dialec.) bank or mound (round a tank).
s.f. A stinking wench (a term of abuse); a coarse, dirty woman.
s.m. A pathic, catamite; a poltroon; an impotent fellow; a term of abuse (without any particular implication); — adj. Impotent, cowardly (cf. gāṅḍū).
s.m. = gaṅḍhīlā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having a smell (whether good or bad); scented, odorous, fragrant; stinking, fœtid; — a scented person, &c.
s.f. 1˚ = gāndī; — 2˚ = gāndhinī, q.v. s.f. Name of a princess of Kāśi (who was the wife of Śvaphalka and mother of Akrūra; — see gāndinī).
s.m. A bow; the bow of Arjun.
wety: From گانْڈ (gānḍ) + ـُو (-ū).

def: s.m. A bow; the bow of Arjun.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Armed with a bow; — an epithet of Arjun.
s.m. A pathic, catamite; a poltroon; an impotent fellow; a term of abuse (without any particular implication); — adj. Impotent, cowardly (cf. gāṅḍū).
s.f. See s.v. gaṇḍ. s.f. = گانڙ gāṅṛ, q.v. s.f. The anus; the privities
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright

def: s.m. A catamite, a sodomite; — an impotent man; a timid person, a coward.
wety: From گانْڈ (gānḍ) + ـُو (-ū).

def: s.m. Wheat
subs: or gandum-rang, adj. Wheat-coloured, tawny, brown, swarthy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گندم (gandom). Doublet of گیہُوں (gehū̃).

def: adj. = gandum-rang, q.v.s.v. gandum.
ex:
جب کہ ایسا ہو گندمی لونڈا
تب گنہگار کیوں نہ ہو آدم
jab ki aisā ho gandumī lauṇḍā
tab gunahgār kyõ na ho ādam

when there is such a wheat-complexioned boy
why then should not Adam be a sinner?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گندمی (gandumī), from گندم (gandum, “wheat”).

def: s.f. A kind of long grass used for thatching (cf. ganaurī).
s.m. See s.v. gaṇ. s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.
s.m. See s.v. gaṇ. s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit गणेश (gaṇeśa).

ety: S. गङ्गा; Pers. also gang, m.
def: s.f. The Ganges; — a river, stream
subs: or gang-bar-āmad, s.f. Alluvial land recovered from a river (esp. from the Ganges)
s.f. Encroachment of the Ganges (or of any other river) by diluvion

def: s.f. The river Ganges (there is also a Ganges in the sky — ākāś-gaṅgā — and one under the earth); — a river or stream (generally)
subs: (for gaṅgā-vāsī), s.m. One who resides near the Ganges
s.m. The further bank of the Ganges, or of any river; the country beyond the Ganges
s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who conducts pilgrims to the Ganges; a Brāhmaṇ priest who officiates by the Ganges; — a man of low caste employed in removing dead bodies
s.f. Dying on the banks of the Ganges
s.m. Bank of the Ganges; — country along the banks of the Ganges
s.f. A vessel in which Ganges-water is kept
'To take up a vessel containing Ganges-water,' to swear on the water of the Ganges

s.f. The Ganges and Jamnā rivers; — a mixture of any kind; — a mode of adjusting the interest of a loan (the debtor being credited with the interest of the instalments paid by him, while he is debited, per contra, with the interest of the full amount on the original loan)

adj. Mixed (as oil and butter); made of mixed metal (as of gold and silver, or of brass and copper); — s.f. A mixture of gold and silver, or of brass and copper; — an ear-ring made of such mixed metal
(ʻthe gate of the Gangeś), s.m. The place (Hardwār) where the Ganges issues from the mountains and enters the plains

s.m. Ganges-supporter'; an epithet of Śiva (according to the legend, the Ganges, in its descent from heaven, first alighted on the head of Śiva, and continued for a long period entangled in his hair); a proper name (of a man)
s.m. The place where the Ganges empties itself into the ocean; the mouths of the Ganges
vulg. gaṅgā-asnān or aśnān, or gaṅgā-nahān, s.m. Bathing in the Ganges (esp. as an act of devotion); — an annual fair held on the Ganges on the last day of Kārttik
s.m. The sacred district of the Ganges, i.e. the river Ganges and the country extending about two kos on either side of its banks (all dying within these limits go to heaven, whatever their crimes)
To die on either bank of the Ganges; to go to heaven
To bathe in the Ganges; to be freed from sin and go to heaven
(˚gā+ut˚), s.f. The source of the Ganges, the place where it springs from the mountains
(˚gā+ud˚), s.m. Ganges water; — water of any sacred river
(˚gā+ūr˚), s.m. The rising of the Ganges (or of any other river) in the rains
s.f. Pilgrimage to, the Ganges; — carrying a sick person to the river-side to die there.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गङ्गा (gaṅgā).

def: s.m. A large metallic vessel for holding water.
s.m. Land subject to inundations of the Ganges.
s.f. A blind boil.
s.m. (dialec.) = gargaiyā = gauraiyā, q.v.
s.f. Ganges-sand.

s.m. corr. of gāhak, q.v. s.m. (f. -ikā), Taker; seizer (e.g. jān-kā gāhak, 'one who takes or aims at life'); receiver, getter; accepter; approver, appreciator (e.g. guṇ-gāhak); — purchaser, buyer, chapman, vendor, dealer.
s.m. corr. of gehūṅ, q.v. s.m. Wheat ( = gohūṅ).
s.f. 'Gunny,' a strong coarse kind of sacking made from the fibres of Corchorus olitorius, and of C. capsularis.
